description: Under restart mode, a trigger firing while a run is mid-delay cancels that run (the evaluating engine itself keeps running throughout — this is mode-driven cancellation, distinct from an engine restart) and starts a fresh run from the top; the fresh run's delay timer starts at zero rather than resuming the canceled run's prior elapsed time.

events.note: run-1 is mid-delay with 4000ms of its 10000ms delay elapsed

expected.reason: the delay resets to not-started rather than resuming from the 4000ms already elapsed on run-1; both runs' timing is monotonic
